note: Corrected ACs — second rewrite addressing all 7 QA objections. AC1 is now end-to-end (OBJ-6). IS_SUBAGENT promoted to Must Have (OBJ-3). Gap C and R4 removed (OBJ-4, OBJ-5). pretool-tool-policy.py addressed (OBJ-1, OBJ-2). Task ID mismatch addressed (OBJ-7).

acceptance_criteria.given: M1 applied (ba.allowed_write_path_prefixes in policies/tool-policy.v1.json includes narrow prefix such as */docs/research/case-), M2 applied (pretool-bash-safety.sh sentinel block no longer gated by IS_SUBAGENT != 1), M3 applied (task ID reader fallback aligned to match writer), AND a valid sentinel grant exists at /tmp/claude-grants/<task_id>.json with {op: /tmp/xlsx-venv/bin/python} for the active task_id
acceptance_criteria.when: ALL PreToolUse hooks that fire for Bash are invoked in sequence against payload {tool_name:Bash, tool_input:{command:/tmp/xlsx-venv/bin/python /root/orchestra/docs/dev/find_red_cells.py > /root/orchestra/docs/research/case-3}, agent_id:agent123, subagent_type:ba, session_id:sid1}
acceptance_criteria.then: each hook exits 0 (no hook returns nonzero); pretool-bash-safety.sh emits SENTINEL_OK; pretool-tool-policy.py exits 0

check.invocation: invoke pretool-bash-safety.sh then pretool-tool-policy.py in sequence with above JSON payload piped via stdin; assert exit_code=0 for both; assert stderr contains SENTINEL_OK from pretool-bash-safety.sh
check.expected_result: exit_code=0 for all hooks in sequence

acceptance_criteria.given: no agent_id in hook payload (main-agent context)
acceptance_criteria.when: pretool-tool-policy.py is invoked with the same Bash command containing redirect to /root/orchestra/docs/research/case-3
acceptance_criteria.then: hook exits 0 unchanged; resolve_agent_type returns None at line 101; no role check runs; behavior unchanged from before M1

acceptance_criteria.given: IS_SUBAGENT=1 (agent_id present) AND valid sentinel at /tmp/claude-grants/<task_id>.json with op=/tmp/xlsx-venv/bin/python for the active task_id
acceptance_criteria.then: the sentinel block is executed (not gated out by IS_SUBAGENT); match_sentinel_grant_for_bash_command returns non-None; hook exits 0 with SENTINEL_OK; AND check_and_consume_allowlist at line 145 still returns 1 immediately for IS_SUBAGENT=1 (legacy grant firewall unchanged)

check.invocation: write sentinel file; invoke pretool-bash-safety.sh with IS_SUBAGENT=1 payload; assert exit_code=0; assert SENTINEL_OK in output; assert line 145 firewall log is present
check.expected_result: exit_code=0, SENTINEL_OK emitted, legacy firewall at line 145 still fires

acceptance_criteria.when: hooks/pretool-tool-policy.py is invoked with ba subagent Bash payload containing redirect to /root/orchestra/docs/research/case-3
writer (userprompt-consent-allowlist.sh) and reader (pretool-bash-safety.sh) both derive TASK_ID
acceptance_criteria.then: both resolve to the same string for all four scenarios: (a) CLAUDE_TASK_ID env present: both use it; (b) CLAUDE_TASK_ID unset, PreToolUse JSON task_id field present differing from session_id: both fall back to session_id (mismatch scenario being fixed); (c) only session_id present: both use it; (d) subagent payload: writer exits without writing (Step 0), reader's IS_SUBAGENT=1 path uses same task_id

check.invocation: for each scenario (a-d): write sentinel under key writer derives; invoke pretool-bash-safety.sh reader path; assert sentinel is found and loaded; assert SENTINEL_NONE does not occur
check.expected_result: writer and reader use identical TASK_ID in all four scenarios

acceptance_criteria.given: a sentinel exists for the current task but does NOT structurally match the Bash command's allowed_operations[]
acceptance_criteria.then: SENTINEL_EXISTS_FOR_TASK=1 is set and legacy /allow path is suppressed; this behavior is unchanged after M1/M2/M3 are applied; CF-1 logic at lines 517-526, 551 is intact

check.invocation: write sentinel with op=unrelated_command; invoke pretool-bash-safety.sh with different Bash command; assert SENTINEL_EXISTS_NO_MATCH path fires; assert legacy allowlist is suppressed
check.expected_result: CF-1 invariant preserved: SENTINEL_EXISTS_FOR_TASK=1 on mismatch; legacy path blocked

acceptance_criteria.given: user issues /allow .* or /allow re:.*python.* (regex/wildcard sentinel; is_regex=true → op: *)
acceptance_criteria.then: EITHER (a) no sentinel file exists for the regex grant (preferred: is_regex=true → skip sentinel write in userprompt-consent-allowlist.sh), OR (b) sentinel has legacy_fallback_allowed: true and is exempted from SENTINEL_EXISTS_FOR_TASK=1; AND legacy check_and_consume_allowlist IS consulted

check.invocation: simulate /allow .* via userprompt-consent-allowlist.sh; check /tmp/claude-grants/<task_id>.json for absence or legacy_fallback_allowed=true; invoke pretool-bash-safety.sh; verify legacy allowlist is consulted and CF-1 does not suppress it
check.expected_result: CF-1 suppression does not fire for regex/wildcard grants; legacy path is consulted
